ActionItems: Hold the standard rate at $95/hr through the slow winter weeks
Keep Darren's hours at 30/week until the spring panel jobs land
BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Kelly Electric LLC monthly break-even, residential and small commercial electrical work in the Philadelphia metro. Single owner-operator plus one apprentice.

CurrentState.Note: InstructorPay_Raj line carries apprentice Darren Yates wage at $22/hr. AaronDrawFromNet is Brandon's owner draw. EquipmentSales is wholesale honey sold through the business.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even runs about 50 billable hours a month at $95/hr. Anything past that funds the owner draw and the SEP-IRA. Winter is tight, summer carries the year.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Add a second apprentice at $20/hr part-time; need roughly 12 more billable hours a month to absorb the cost.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Tighter margin in slow months, more capacity for panel upgrades in spring.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Add part-time second apprentice

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Higher fixed cost; only worth it with steady commercial contracts.
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Rent dedicated shop and storage unit
